agent_notes: Lewisham has a strong Deptford/Brockley drinks cluster and credible market/galleries, but very few true Books & Coffee venues under PAINTS v6.
Creative Walks are limited to Beckenham Place Park and Blackheath because those are the only Lewisham-relevant routes on the PAINTS safety list.
Group walk-in acceptance, Google ratings/review counts, and bank-holiday opening must be host-confirmed before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: Tue-Thu 4pm-11pm, Fri 4pm-midnight, Sat noon-midnight, Sun noon-11pm; Mon closed/open bank holidays per venue site.
venues.walk_in_policy: Venue site says open for bookings and walk-ins; booking page says booking is not essential and most tables are kept free for walk-ins. Group of 8-12: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely with early arrival or heads-up; bar is drinks-only and keeps walk-in tables, but 8-12 must be confirmed.
venues.capacity_notes: Former funeral parlour taproom; limited food, allows delivered pizza/outside tables per site.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday 2pm-5pm or Sunday afternoon before evening peak.
venues.why_fits_paints: Small-producer wine and beer in a former funeral parlour gives it a conversational hook without feeling themed. It is local, calm, and specific enough for strangers to bring objects, stories, or sketches. The walk-in policy is unusually clear for this category.
venues.anecdote_story: Launched by the owners of nearby Salthouse bottle shop; Time Out notes the former funeral-parlour setting and focus on small/local producers.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Natural wine, craft beer, small producer taps, spirits, cold snacks; outside food allowed.
venues.safety_notes: Brockley Road is active and transit-adjacent; evening departure to Brockley/Crofton Park should still be host-checked.
venues.risks_watchouts: Small room; group of 12 may need a heads-up despite walk-ins.

venues.opening_hours: Mon closed; Tue-Thu 16:00-23:00; Fri 14:00-midnight; Sat noon-midnight; Sun 15:00-21:00 per venue site.
venues.walk_in_policy: Site foregrounds booking; walk-ins for 8-12 are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm. Cap retained as Tier A only because operational hours and category fit are strong; host should call before listing.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely possible off-peak if arranged; must be confirmed.
venues.capacity_notes: Neighbourhood wine bar and shop with food; intimate rather than large-format.
venues.why_fits_paints: It has the right mix of owner-led wine knowledge, local suppliers, and neighbourhood warmth. The natural/organic wine angle supports show-and-tell conversation without turning the event into a formal tasting. Crofton Park also balances the Deptford-heavy list.
venues.anecdote_story: Jerome Bichot is described by the venue as French-born and hospitality-trained, opening the bar from a long-held dream.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Natural and organic wines, pinsa pizza, cheese and local supplier products.
venues.safety_notes: Main-road location near Crofton Park rail; evening route still needs host check.
venues.backup_walk_time: 10 min walk north on Brockley Road
venues.risks_watchouts: Walk-ins for a group are not explicitly guaranteed online.

venues.opening_hours: CAMRA London lists Mon noon-midnight, Tue-Thu 11am-midnight, Fri 11am-1am, Sat 9am-1am, Sun 9am-midnight.
venues.walk_in_policy: Instagram says no bookings needed; group of 8-12 still UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Better for an informal pub group than a quiet tasting; early evening or weekend afternoon advised.
venues.capacity_notes: Historic split-level pub expanded next door in 2022; steps noted by CAMRA.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday 2pm-5pm; avoid quiz/folk peaks if conversation is central.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is Deptford texture rather than generic pub convenience: old waterside history, ale culture, annual rituals, and a backstreet feel. It gives attendees a real local story. The venue is robust operationally and walk-in-coded.
venues.anecdote_story: CAMRA notes a pub of this name has occupied the back street near the Thames for several hundred years and that it won Lewisham District Pub of the Year in 2023, 2024 and 2025.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Cask ales, guest beers, Belgian bottles, pub food, folk nights, quiz nights, Pickle Festival culture.
venues.safety_notes: Quiet backstreet near river; use lit route to Deptford/Deptford High Street stations after dark.
venues.risks_watchouts: Not wine-led; conversation may compete with pub events.

venues.opening_hours: Venue/Facebook/press indicate Deptford openings focused on Fri-Sat and events; exact July 2026 standard hours UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Reservations/private hire available; walk-ins for 8-12 are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm. Tier B due to operational uncertainty and popularity.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Possible if area reservation is accepted; not safe to assume walk-in group seating.
venues.capacity_notes: Multiple Deptford rooms/arches; free private hire for large SE London resident groups noted on site, but PAINTS-sized walk-in needs confirmation.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday afternoon rather than late night.
venues.why_fits_paints: It is almost too theatrical, but the autobiographical decor and Deptford comeback story are genuinely distinctive. It works for a playful show-and-tell night where objects and nostalgia are the point. Operationally it needs more host intervention than PAINTS ideally wants.
venues.anecdote_story: Founder Tristan opened it in 2013 using his late grandmother's furniture, ornaments and crockery; the bar later returned to Deptford after a pop-up period.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Teapot cocktails, kitsch living-room interiors, themed rooms, nostalgic crockery and fabric work.
venues.safety_notes: Deptford Market Yard/Resolution Way has footfall; late-night dispersal should use main station routes.
venues.risks_watchouts: High obviousness and booking/queue risk; may overpower quieter PAINTS formats.

venues.opening_hours: Tue-Thu 5pm-10pm, Fri 1pm-11pm, Sat noon-11pm, Sun 4pm-8pm, Mon closed per venue cocktail page.
venues.walk_in_policy: Instagram says booking recommended and walk-ins welcome; group of 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely if early or with heads-up; outside Market Yard seating helps in fair weather.
venues.capacity_notes: Railway arch bar in Deptford Market Yard; limited indoor seating plus external seating noted by beer guide.
venues.why_fits_paints: The vodka infusions and future micro-distillery story give it a material craft angle. It is tucked into the restored Deptford arches, which suits PAINTS' slightly off-grid feel. It is more casual than poetic, so Tier B.
venues.anecdote_story: CAMRA notes the bar occupies an arch under the Grade II-listed Deptford station carriage ramp restored in 2015.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Vodka infusions, cocktails, craft beer, packaged beer, Deptford Arches Distillery identity.
venues.safety_notes: Very close to Deptford station; use main arch/station route at night.
venues.risks_watchouts: Could feel more drinks-out than cultural unless the host frames the infusion/distillery story.

venues.opening_hours: Thu 6pm-11pm, Fri 5pm-11:30pm, Sat 1pm-11:30pm, Sun 1pm-9pm per venue site.
venues.walk_in_policy: Venue site says plenty of walk-in space inside/outside and large group bookings up to 34 people.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong operationally; group still should be checked for peak Saturday.
venues.capacity_notes: Taproom with indoor/outdoor space and explicit large-group booking note.
venues.why_fits_paints: It is less intimate than a wine bar, but the brewery-at-source format is reliable and sociable. The arch setting keeps it local rather than generic. It is a good operational fallback for Deptford drinks.
venues.anecdote_story: Villages started in Deptford in 2016 and operates from Resolution Way arches.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Fresh brewery beers, taproom pours, selected wines/ciders.
venues.safety_notes: Resolution Way/Deptford station area has active footfall; late night host should keep the group on main lit paths.
venues.risks_watchouts: Beer-led, so it misses the natural-wine bonus signals.

venues.opening_hours: Thu 18:00-23:30, Fri 18:00-02:00, Sat 17:00-02:00, Sun 13:30-18:00, Mon-Wed closed per venue site.
venues.walk_in_policy: Booking page exists; walk-ins for 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm. Late DJ/nightclub profile makes it Tier B.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Best only for early evening before DJ/dance programming.
venues.capacity_notes: Bar, restaurant and events space under Deptford rail station.
venues.why_fits_paints: Its Jamaican family story and arch location are stronger than a normal cocktail bar. The creative-space identity fits PAINTS, but late-night music makes conversation less reliable. Use as a textured fallback, not a default first pick.
venues.anecdote_story: Founder Gordon McGowan has described the venue as shaped by his Mandeville, Jamaica childhood; the name nods to Alexander Bustamante.
venues.safety_notes: Under Deptford station arches; avoid late-night dispersal without a clear host plan.
venues.risks_watchouts: Can become too loud/nightlife-led for show-and-tell.

venues.opening_hours: Mon closed; Tue 5pm-10pm; Wed 5pm-11pm; Thu 5pm-midnight; Fri-Sat 4pm-midnight; Sun 2pm-8pm per venue site.
venues.walk_in_policy: Walk-in handling for 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Possible off-peak; rear/semi-private area noted by hire listings but not a walk-in guarantee.
venues.capacity_notes: Cocktail/beer/wine bar with three spaces according to venue-hire source.
venues.why_fits_paints: It is a practical neighbourhood bar with a stronger local drinks list than its frontage suggests. It is useful as a Brockley backup to Joyce or Jerome's. It lacks a deep cultural hook, so Tier B.
venues.anecdote_story: SquareMeal describes it as a local-booze neighbourhood asset with drinks from Peckham, Bermondsey and Deptford producers.
venues.safety_notes: Main road between Brockley and Crofton Park; use main routes to stations.
venues.risks_watchouts: More operational than magical; host framing needed.

venues.opening_hours: Saturdays 10am-2pm per official site and Lewisham Council.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; meeting-point coffee walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for dispersed browsing and regrouping; use a fixed entrance/coffee meet.
venues.capacity_notes: Outdoor food and produce market in Lewisham College car park.
venues.why_fits_paints: It is a proper south-east London food market with maker/producer density rather than a generic street-food lineup. The car-park setting is unglamorous in the right PAINTS way: people talk to stallholders, compare finds, and leave with stories. It is operationally simple and open-access.
venues.anecdote_story: Visit London highlights named traders such as Mons Cheesemongers, Pasta di Grazia, Aston's Bakehouse, Mother Flipper and Mike + Ollie.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Produce, cheese, bread, pasta, flowers, prepared food and drinks.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime market with strong local footfall; standard urban pickpocket awareness.
venues.backup_walk_time: 15 min by bus/transit, host to verify live route
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather exposed; no-frills site may surprise attendees expecting a prettier market.

venues.opening_hours: Wed, Fri, Sat 9am-5:30pm; road closed 6am-7pm on market days per Lewisham Council.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; meeting-point coffee walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for wandering, but host needs a strong route and rendezvous points.
venues.capacity_notes: Large public street market; mixed goods, food and household stalls.
venues.why_fits_paints: It has real Deptford density, multicultural texture, and old market energy. It is less curated than PAINTS' ideal, so the host must shape the route carefully. Use when the goal is place-story rather than polished makers.
venues.anecdote_story: Lewisham Council describes the market as selling fresh fish, fruit and vegetables, multi-cultural food, haberdashery, clothing, fabric and household items.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Food stalls, produce, textiles, bric-a-brac, high-street shops and cafes.
venues.safety_notes: Busy daytime high street; avoid late dusk dispersal and keep valuables close.
venues.backup_walk_time: 15 min by bus/transit, host to verify live route
venues.risks_watchouts: Not sufficiently curated for Tier A; Saturday crowding can fragment a group.

venues.opening_hours: Sundays 10am-3pm; free/drop-in in Horniman Gardens per official event page.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; Horniman Cafe open 9am-5pm daily per plan-your-visit page, but group walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good if group meets at museum/cafe and browses in pairs.
venues.capacity_notes: Market in museum gardens with seating, wheelchair access and assistance animals welcome per official page.
venues.why_fits_paints: The market is embedded in one of south London's most characterful museum gardens, so the day can move from stalls to objects to skyline views. It has maker/food identity and a strong cultural anchor. The setting is weather-exposed but more resilient than a plain street market.
venues.anecdote_story: The Horniman itself opened from Frederick Horniman's collections; the market sits beside a museum known for its gardens and eclectic holdings.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Food traders, non-food traders, museum gardens, cafe, nearby collections.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime family/cultural setting; hills and garden paths may affect accessibility.
venues.backup_venue: Beckenham Place Park Food & Farmers' Market
venues.backup_walk_time: 15 min by rail/bus may exceed in live conditions - host must verify
venues.risks_watchouts: Backup is not comfortably within 15 minutes in all live routes; demote if transit is disrupted.

venues.opening_hours: Last Sunday of the month 10:00-16:00 per Food Market Company; Instagram snippet also says last Sunday, 10-3/10-4.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; meeting point UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Works for casual food browsing; choose a precise station-side meeting point.
venues.capacity_notes: Monthly outdoor food market between Catford/Catford Bridge stations.
venues.why_fits_paints: Catford gives the borough list a useful counterweight and the market is small enough to keep a group together. It is community-forward rather than destination-polished. Tier B because monthly cadence and backup options are weaker.
venues.anecdote_story: Food Market Company says it took on Catford after operating Beckenham Place Park and South Norwood markets.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Street food and local food traders; exact trader mix varies.
venues.safety_notes: Station-adjacent daytime location; standard crowd and valuables awareness.
venues.backup_venue: Beckenham Place Park Food & Farmers' Market
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 15 min by rail/bus if services align - host must verify live
venues.risks_watchouts: Monthly schedule and weather exposure; live date confirmation required.

venues.venue_name: Beckenham Place Park Food & Farmers' Market
venues.opening_hours: Weekly Sunday market 10am-3pm indicated by venue/Instagram/Tripadvisor sources; live Sunday dates must be confirmed.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; mansion/cafe meeting point walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a 2-3 hour market plus park/mansion wander.
venues.capacity_notes: Outdoor park market with local trader and produce focus.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the strongest market-walk hybrid in Lewisham: a producer market inside a PAINTS-approved park. It has enough place-story, open space, and food texture for strangers to talk naturally. Use in daylight and away from the lake edge.
venues.anecdote_story: The market launched in March 2022 and is described as family-run with a local trader/produce focus.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Farmers' market produce, hot food, breads, cheese, flowers, mansion and parkland.
venues.safety_notes: Beckenham Place Park is on the PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist. Lake area has a recent serious safety caveat; route must avoid unsupervised water edge.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 15 min by rail/bus if services align - host must verify live
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather and transport; lake safety must be explicitly briefed.

venues.opening_hours: Galleries open Wed-Sun noon-6pm; Sly-tone cafe Mon-Fri 10am-4pm per official contact page.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free public gallery; timed-entry not indicated for FLARE-UP. Group walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong for a slow-look gallery day; call ahead for group etiquette/access needs.
venues.capacity_notes: Contemporary art gallery on Goldsmiths campus; free to all.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the standout Lewisham gallery pick because the current exhibition itself justifies the gathering. FLARE-UP offers real emotional, political and aesthetic depth for a group to discuss. It is also close to New Cross transit and other artist-led backups.
venues.anecdote_story: The gallery opened in 2018 in a former Victorian bathhouse/water-tank complex redesigned by Assemble; current show is co-curated around illness, disability, neurodivergence and Deafness.
venues.serve_or_on_view: FLARE-UP, 21 May-16 August 2026: sculpture, installation, painting, film, poetry, music and performance.
venues.safety_notes: Campus/New Cross Gate route is active; after-dark route to station should use main streets.
venues.risks_watchouts: Exhibition topic may need sensitive host framing; cafe open days differ from gallery days.

venues.opening_hours: Museum daily 10am-5:30pm; gardens 7:15am/8am Sundays and bank holidays to 8:30pm; cafe 9am-5pm daily per official pages.
venues.walk_in_policy: Museum free/drop-in; Voyage to the Deep is ticketed, so timed-entry/group availability UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for daytime museum-garden hybrid if ticketed exhibition has availability.
venues.capacity_notes: Large museum/gardens with cafe, kiosks and accessible visitor infrastructure.
venues.best_time_slot: Sunday 10:30am-1pm, optionally paired with Horniman Market.
venues.why_fits_paints: Horniman is photographable, eccentric and generous, but the current headline exhibition is family-skewed rather than a grown-up PAINTS art anchor. The permanent collections and gardens still support a strong social route. Tier B because the gallery backup radius is weak and ticketing needs confirmation.
venues.anecdote_story: Frederick Horniman opened his collections to the public in the late 19th century; the museum is known for anthropology, music, natural history and gardens.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Voyage to the Deep - Underwater Adventures until 1 November 2026; permanent collections, aquarium, gardens and market.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime cultural site; garden slopes/paths need accessibility awareness.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying gallery/museum backup found within 10 min - closest cultural alternative requires transit.
venues.risks_watchouts: Ticketed exhibition and family audience; weak same-category backup.

venues.opening_hours: Thu-Sun noon-5pm; current Grids exhibition 2-12 July 2026 per official site.
venues.walk_in_policy: Gallery opening implies drop-in; group walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable for a short artist-led gallery stop; check private-view or install days.
venues.capacity_notes: Charity studio complex and contemporary gallery in Deptford Creek premises.
venues.why_fits_paints: APT has the right artist-led, working-studio credibility for Lewisham. The current exhibition is live and close to Deptford creative infrastructure. It is Tier B because press validation is thin and exhibition depth needs host preview.
venues.anecdote_story: Art in Perpetuity Trust was founded in 1995 by artists in an old warehouse and now provides 42 studios, gallery, sculpture yard and external performance space.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Grids, 2-12 July 2026, alongside creative writing by Georgia Anna Bloom.
venues.safety_notes: Creekside is fine in daytime; avoid isolated creekside wandering after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 12 min walk; exceeds PAINTS 10-min ideal, so primary remains Tier B
venues.risks_watchouts: Backup is outside strict radius and may not have a current exhibition.

venues.venue_name: Beckenham Place Park Mansion, Woods and Lake Route
venues.opening_hours: Pedestrians can access the park at all times per Lewisham Council; lake area is ticketed and open 7am-6pm per park site.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access park route; Mansion/cafe/weather backup walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good in daylight with a clear host route and no unsupervised water-edge gathering.
venues.capacity_notes: Large park; meeting point should be Mansion courtyard or station-side entrance.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is Lewisham's strongest sanctioned Creative Walk: ancient woodland, mansion, market potential and big-sky parkland. Stops can include the Mansion, walled garden/pleasure grounds, ancient woodland, and lake viewpoint from a safe distance. It rewards attention without requiring participants to be hikers.
venues.anecdote_story: The park traces to John Cator's 18th-century estate and later moved from golf-course landscape to restored public parkland; Heritage Fund backed major restoration.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Creative-walk stops: Mansion/courtyard, restored pleasure grounds, ancient woodland, lake viewpoint, Sunday market if open.
venues.safety_notes: Park is on PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist. Recent fatal lake incident means route must avoid water-edge lingering and use daylight only.
venues.backup_walk_time: Within 5 min of Mansion start point
venues.risks_watchouts: Water safety; host must check lake restrictions, toilets, weather and cafe hours.

venues.venue_name: Blackheath Village and Heath Edge Route
venues.opening_hours: Open common/heath; temporary toilets 11am-8pm per Lewisham Council. Daylight route only.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access route; cafe/weather backup walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable for daylight edge-route; avoid crossing wide open heath after dark.
venues.capacity_notes: Outdoor route; meeting point at Blackheath station or village cafe.
venues.why_fits_paints: Blackheath gives Lewisham a historical, atmospheric walk without inventing a new unsafe park. Stops can include the station/village edge, All Saints sightline, heath commons history, and views toward Greenwich. Tier B because it is exposed, shared with Greenwich, and weather-backup evidence is softer.
venues.anecdote_story: Lewisham Council notes Blackheath has been held in trust for public benefit since 1871; Greenwich history sources describe its highwaymen/fair/recreation past.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Creative-walk stops: Blackheath station/village, Lewisham-managed heath edge, All Saints exterior/viewpoint, route toward Greenwich Park boundary without entering unsourced paths.
venues.safety_notes: Blackheath is on PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist. Daylight only; avoid isolated open sections after dark.
venues.backup_venue: Village cafe/pub weather stop near Blackheath station
venues.backup_walk_time: Within 5 min of station start point; exact venue UNVERIFIED - host must confirm
venues.risks_watchouts: Exposed weather, shared borough boundary and weaker backup certainty.

venues.opening_hours: Bookshop Crawl lists Mon-Sat 10am-5pm and Sun 11am-4pm; official site confirms address and organisation but hours need host verification.
venues.walk_in_policy: Retail walk-in likely; group of 8-12 and coffee-shop/on-site coffee status UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Only suitable if the shop agrees to a quiet group visit and coffee provision is confirmed.
venues.capacity_notes: Award-winning children's/inclusive bookshop; not a guaranteed two-hour sit-down cafe.
venues.best_time_slot: Sunday 11am-1pm or weekday morning if approved by shop.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the only Lewisham books candidate with enough literary mission to merit inclusion despite operational softness. Its equality-in-children's-books focus gives real story depth. It should be treated as a host-confirmed pilot, not an automatically publishable venue.
venues.anecdote_story: Moon Lane Ink grew out of Tales on Moon Lane and works to address inequity of access, representation and roles in children's publishing.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Inclusive children's books, events/workshops, community bookshop mission; coffee provision reported in local video/social snippets but not robustly verified.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime high-street setting; group should avoid blocking retail flow.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying Books & Coffee backup found within 10 min - nearest cultural alternative is Horniman Museum/Cafe, not same category.
venues.risks_watchouts: Biggest category-risk pick: on-site coffee and group seating are not sufficiently verified.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Former Catford venue appears to have relocated to Bromley; not a current Lewisham venue despite older Catford listings.
Excellent natural-wine fit, but current official web presence is mainly social/search snippets and same-category backup within 10 minutes was not strong enough for inclusion.
Strong historic Brockley wine story, but current status/hours are contradictory across sources, with Time Out marking it closed and other listings indicating past/changed operation.
Market is listed by Lewisham Council, but it is in Telegraph Hill Lower Park, which is not on the PAINTS safety list; brief says park meeting-point logic must follow the curated safety list.
Artist-led building is very PAINTS-coded, but the live/current exhibition evidence for 4 July 2026 was not strong enough; recent listed show had ended in June.
Good community gallery backup for New Cross, but no sufficiently clear current exhibition/opening-hours package for a primary PAINTS listing.
Important visual-arts festival, but 2025 biennial programme was not a current July 2026 venue/exhibition suitable for this longlist.
Excellent secondhand/vintage bookshop, but no verified on-site coffee or literal next-door coffee partner; fails Books & Coffee raised bar.
Longstanding bookshop with events, but no on-site cafe/coffee partner literally next door; fails Books & Coffee requirement despite strong literary identity.
Culturally strong, but located inside Lewisham Shopping Centre, and PAINTS v6 rejects malls/shopping-centre venues for Tier A style sourcing.
